summary: Ukrainian FPV drones are targeting Russian logistics near Donetsk, with a "carousel" of seven drones operating near Peski, about 25-30 km from the city. Russian forces are countering with fiber-optic FPV drones like "Barmaley," which destroyed an American MaxxPro armored vehicle in Dobropole. The "Rubicon" Center reported 162 enemy targets destroyed on March 10, 2026, including strikes on Ukrainian personnel and FPV air defense against fixed-wing UAVs. Russian operators note that enemy "Molniya" drones are increasingly difficult to counter due to unknown control frequencies, and the recent decline in Shahed drone attacks may be linked to production issues, intensified electronic warfare, and the loss of Starlink connectivity.
